tasks.task: Understand and practice the concept of 'zone of proximal development' by stepping out of your comfort zone.
tasks.description: Try a language or technology you haven't used before, take on challenging projects with no immediate tangible return.

tasks.task: Read actual documentation instead of just quick start guides and actively seek deeper knowledge about the tools you're using.
tasks.description: Engage in reading material beyond what is required by your current job, dive into books like 'The Pragmatic Programmer' or 'Fundamentals of Software Architecture'.

tasks.task: Experiment and break things on purpose to understand why they work the way they do. Ask yourself questions like 'What happens if I remove this line?'
tasks.description: Build side scripts, try different scenarios with your code, and learn from undesirable difficulties.

tasks.task: Volunteer for projects that are not easy or obvious to tackle. This helps you face challenges head-on.
tasks.description: Offer assistance where it is needed most, even if the task seems trivial at first.

tasks.task: Seek feedback from open-source communities and contribute to projects that align with your standards for quality code.
tasks.description: Contribute to issues or documentation of projects you use regularly. This can range from fixing minor bugs to offering thoughtful comments on pull requests.

tasks.task: Teach what you've learned by explaining concepts in writing, verbally, or through a video to someone else.
tasks.description: Record explanations of complex topics for classmate, co-worker, or online. This process will help solidify your understanding and expose any gaps.

tasks.task: Explore Superbase for setting up a backend using free open-source software and integrate PG Vector into the database.
tasks.description: Sign up for free on Superbase or self-host using Docker. Explore its features like off storage, Postgres database, real-time functions, etc.
